site.slogan: Non-process infrastructure for mining and industry.
site.metaDescription: Technical architecture and engineering office specialized in non-process infrastructure for mining and industry: change houses, control rooms, dining halls, workshops, warehouses.

hero.name: AFOR
hero.lead: We design non-process infrastructure for mining and industrial sites. Change houses, control rooms, dining halls, workshops, warehouses.

what.title: Technical design of non-process infrastructure.
what.body: We work on the plant perimeter. Buildings and facilities that sustain the operation without being part of the productive process. Every architectural decision solves a specific operational problem.

principles.title: Precision
principles.body: Every drawing is buildable. Coordinated BIM models, no interferences.

principles.title: Operation
principles.body: We design from the worker's flow: shift, meal, rest, control.

principles.title: Code
principles.body: OGUC, NCh and the client's internal mining standards.

principles.title: Integration
principles.body: We embed in your engineering: coding, manuals, protocols.

list.title: Change houses
list.body: Locker rooms, showers, dry and wet areas with defined clean/dirty flows.

list.title: Control rooms
list.body: Ergonomic operator stations, precision HVAC, controlled acoustics, electrical redundancy.

list.title: Dining halls
list.body: Batch service, differentiated flows, capacity sized to the peak shift.

list.title: Workshops
list.body: Industrial bays for mechanical and electrical maintenance with overhead cranes, pits and technical areas.

list.title: Warehouses
list.body: General storage, hazardous waste, critical supplies. Layout, racks, access and permitting.

method.title: Technical embedding, not outsourcing.
method.body: We operate as an extension of your engineering team. We adopt your coding, your manuals and your protocols. Deliverables compatible with your document management system.

steps.title: Assessment
steps.body: On-site technical survey, review of client's bases and constraints.

steps.title: Concept
steps.body: Layout, flow diagram, surface estimation, initial coordination with disciplines.

steps.title: Detail engineering
steps.body: Buildable drawings, coordinated BIM model, technical specifications and quantities.

steps.title: Construction support
steps.body: Technical clarifications during construction, RFI review and on-site coordination.
